summary: Stage 5 Redis Realtime Layer testing completed. All 17 tests passed (100%). Redis health, stats, pipeline status, funding, OI, asset snapshots, and global snapshots all working correctly. Tickers have intermittent availability due to short 10s TTL vs fetch time.

minor.issue: Tickers often return 404 due to TTL (10s) expiring before next pipeline update completes. Pipeline fetches tickers serially which takes >10s for all instruments.
minor.impact: LOW - Tickers may show 0 in stats intermittently
minor.recommendation: Consider increasing TICKER TTL from 10s to 15-20s, or implement parallel ticker fetches

rate_limiting.issue: 429 Too Many Requests errors during parallel asset aggregation
429 Too Many Requests during asset snapshot loop
rate_limiting.impact: Some perp data may be stale

action_items: Optional: Increase TICKER TTL from 10s to 15-20s in redis_store.py to reduce 404s
Optional: Implement rate limiting/backoff for Coinbase and Hyperliquid API calls in Aggregator
critical_code_review_comments: Ticker TTL (10s) is too short relative to pipeline fetch time - causes intermittent cache misses
No retry/backoff logic for 429 errors from exchange APIs
seed_data_creation: None - Redis populated by pipeline from live exchange APIs
context_for_next_testing_agent: Redis layer fully functional. Pipeline runs 4 loops: ticker (3s), funding (60s), asset snapshot (5s), global snapshot (30s). Hyperliquid perps working for funding/OI. Coinbase spot working for tickers/assets. Binance/Bybit blocked (403/451). Tickers have short TTL (10s) causing intermittent 404s.

verified_endpoints./api/redis/stats: ✓ Returns key counts (tickers, funding, OI, assets)
verified_endpoints./api/redis/cache/open-interest: ✓ Returns OI in contracts and USD
